# UC-22.2.12 · NIS2 Multi-Factor Authentication and Secure Communications

## Description

Flags user cohorts whose sign-in telemetry lacks multi-factor indicators by stitching Entra ID `authenticationRequirement` fields, Okta factor claims, and Windows security events into one rolling coverage statistic split between administrators and everyone else.

## Value

Article 21(2)(j) expectations around resilient authentication translate poorly into vague policy PDFs; showing month-over-month MFA coverage by role gives executives and regulators a quantitative story and tells incident responders where takeover risk concentrates.

## Implementation

(1) Land `azure:aad:signin` + `okta:log` into `index=auth`. (2) Normalize Okta `legacyEventType` if still on OktaIM2. (3) Require 100% MFA for `is_admin=1`, 80% general users — tune via `nis2_mfa_thresholds.csv`. (4) Pipe exceptions through GRC register. (5) Integrate PagerDuty for breach of admin thresholds.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Dedicated `index=auth` receiving only identity-provider events to simplify GDPR access reviews; retention 400 days.
• Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) configured with `azure_mgmt` + Graph sign-in modular inputs; verify under **Splunk Web → Apps → Microsoft Cloud Services → Inputs** that poll interval is 300 seconds max to limit throttling.
• Splunk Add-on for Okta ([Splunkbase 6553](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6553)) streaming API key via **Okta Admin → Security → API → Tokens** with `okta.logs.read` scope.
• Splunk Add-on for Windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) collecting Tier-0 `WinEventLog:Security` used only for admin correlation.
• Role `identity_soc` with `srchIndexesAllowed=auth;windows` and no `_audit` delete rights.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Map Graph JSON fields using `props.conf` `FIELDALIAS-authenticationRequirement = authenticationRequirement AS authenticationRequirement` (noop) and `EVAL-user = userPrincipalName` when missing.
• For Okta, ensure `transaction.type` sign-in events include `outcome.result`; drop health-check noise with `TRANSFORMS-setnull` on `debugContext` ping.
• Volume planning: expect 0.5–2 events per user per day; multiply by workforce size.
• Document emergency communications controls (Teams, Webex) separately — this UC covers authentication posture, not call encryption.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Adopt `spl` field. Add admin-only alert `| where is_admin=1 AND mfa_pct < 100` scheduled every 30 minutes.
Optional CIM acceleration path: build a summary `summary_mfa_daily` via `collect` for board reporting.

Step 3 — Validate
• Pick five admins: manually verify Entra portal sign-ins show MFA method within same hour bucket as Splunk.
• For Okta, compare `actor.alternateId` count vs **Reports → SAML usage** for the day.
• Measure parser lag `| eval lag=_indextime-_time | stats p95(lag) by sourcetype` — >900s triggers forwarder inspection.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard with dual threshold lines and CSV export for IAM managers.
• SOAR playbook: disable accounts listed in alert after HR ticked off in ServiceNow (out of scope but referenced).
• Archive compliance exports with `evidenceSigning` quarterly.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`mfa_used` always 0 for Entra** — `authenticationRequirement` missing because TA version <4.6; upgrade Splunkbase 3110.
• **Duplicate users** — UPN vs sAMAccountName mismatch; unify via `ldap_group` lookup.
• **Alert storms during company-wide password reset** — enable maintenance window lookup `nis2_change_freeze.csv`.
• **Okta rate limiting** — switch to Okta System Log streaming (`https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/api/system-log/`).


## SPL

```spl
index=auth OR index=windows earliest=-24h
  (sourcetype IN ("azure:aad:signin","okta:log","OktaIM2:log","WinEventLog:Security"))
| eval user=coalesce(userPrincipalName, actor.alternateId, Account_Name, user)
| eval mfa_used=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)mfa|multifactor|two.?factor|2fa|totp|fido|webauthn|push.*approve"), 1,
    match(lower(authenticationRequirement),"(?i)multi"), 1,
    match(lower(factor),"(?i)push|totp|sms|call|webauthn"), 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval is_admin=if(match(lower(user),"(?i)admin|svc-|service|root|system") OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)privileged|admin.*role"), 1, 0)
| stats count sum(mfa_used) as mfa_count by user, is_admin, sourcetype
| eval mfa_pct=round(100*mfa_count/count, 1)
| where (is_admin=1 AND mfa_pct < 100) OR (is_admin=0 AND mfa_pct < 80)
| sort is_admin, mfa_pct
| table user, is_admin, count, mfa_count, mfa_pct, sourcetype
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Gauge for admin MFA, clustered bar by sourcetype, table of worst offenders, trendline overlay.
